trade.summary: Substantial bilateral trade boosted by CETA. Canada exports crude oil, natural gas, agricultural products, and aerospace goods to the Netherlands. Netherlands exports chemicals, machinery, semiconductors (ASML-linked supply chains), and refined petroleum to Canada.

diplomatic.summary: Exceptionally warm relations rooted in WWII liberation (Canada liberated the Netherlands in 1945 — a bond still celebrated today). Both are NATO allies and G7-adjacent partners. Close cooperation on rule-based order, Ukraine support, and multilateral issues. Regular high-level engagement.

military.summary: Very close NATO allies with deep historical military ties from WWII. Joint exercises, intelligence sharing, and equipment interoperability. Canada and Netherlands cooperate closely in NATO, particularly in Europe's eastern flank and maritime security.

financial.summary: Substantial bilateral investment. Dutch companies (Shell, Unilever, Philips, ASML supply chain) have major Canadian operations. Canadian pension funds invest in Dutch and broader European infrastructure through Netherlands-based funds.

energy.summary: Canada exports LNG and crude oil to Netherlands (Rotterdam). Shell has major Canadian upstream investments. Growing cooperation on hydrogen and clean energy transition.

scientific.summary: Very strong scientific partnership. NSERC-NWO programs cover physics, life sciences, and environmental research. Collaboration in water management, agriculture, and AI. Strong academic exchange program.
